title: Debug a StatefulSet
text:
This task shows you how to debug a StatefulSet.

You need to have a Kubernetes cluster, and the kubectl command-line tool must be configured to communicate with your cluster.
You should have a StatefulSet running that you want to investigate.

In order to list all the pods which belong to a StatefulSet, which have a label
app.kubernetes.io/name=MyApp
set on them,
you can use the following:
kubectl get pods -l app.kubernetes.io/name
=
MyApp
If you find that any Pods listed are in
Unknown
or
Terminating
state for an extended period of time,
refer to the
Deleting StatefulSet Pods
task for
instructions on how to deal with them.
You can debug individual Pods in a StatefulSet using the
Debugging Pods
guide.
